body.text: Crypto News Bot — Daily Digest
Stablecoin market cap has slipped ~$10B since its May peak as GENIUS Act rules near their July 18 finalization deadline.
Stablecoin News
GENIUS Act rules due July 18
Six federal agencies are in the final stretch to finalize GENIUS Act stablecoin rules by the July 18 statutory deadline, including a $5M capital floor for issuers.
Circle wins OCC bank charter
Circle received OCC approval to operate as a national trust bank, letting it directly manage USDC reserves; shares surged on the news.
Visa, BlackRock back rival 'Open USD' stablecoin
Over 140 companies launched a fee-free, shared-governance dollar stablecoin, prompting Circle's stock to fall more than 10% on competitive concerns.
Stablecoin market cap contracts $10B since May
Total stablecoin supply is down roughly 3% from its peak; analysts say the pullback is mild compared to prior bear-market contractions.
Hacking & Security Incidents
'Ill Bloom' flaw drains $5M+ from wallets
Coinspect disclosed a weak-randomness bug in recovery-phrase generation across five wallet implementations; confirmed losses now exceed $5 million.
Hundreds of MetaMask/EVM wallets drained
A wave of fake 'wallet update' prompts tricked users into signing malicious transactions, draining hundreds of EVM-based wallets.
White-hat researchers flag $70B Aptos risk
Hexens researchers found an Aptos consensus flaw with a near-90% exploit success rate and attack costs of just hundreds of dollars; patched before abuse.
Q2 2026 named worst quarter ever for crypto hacks
Crypto losses hit a record 70 separate exploits in Q2, per new industry data, even as total dollar losses stayed below prior-year peaks.
